content.problemStatement: Given **N** points on a 2D plane and an integer **K**, choose 3 distinct points so that the area of the triangle whose vertices are the chosen points does not exceed **K**/2. If there are multiple such triangles, among all possible triangles choose the one which has maximum area.
constraints: No two points will have the same coordinates
No three points will be collinear
content.inputFormat: The first line contains two integers N and K, where N is the number of points and K is the maximum allowed area. Each of the next N lines contains two integers Xi and Yi, denoting the coordinates of the i-th point.
content.outputFormat: Output a single integer, the maximum area of a triangle which does not exceed K/2, multiplied by 2. If there's no valid triangle output -1 instead.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There are 4 triangles in total, and their areas are: 1, 1, 2 and 4. The ones which do not exceed 7/2 are 1, 1 and 2 so the biggest area is 2, and since we are required to multiply the area by 2 before outputting it, it becomes 4.
The only triangle has an area equal to 9/2, which is more than 7/2. So the answer is -1.
The maximum area of a triangle which does not exceed K/2 is 377512147740, so the output is 755024295480.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a brute force approach by calculating the area of all possible triangles and keeping track of the maximum area that does not exceed K/2. The area of a triangle with vertices (x1, y1), (x2, y2), and (x3, y3) can be calculated using the formula: area = |(x1*(y2-y3) + x2*(y3-y1) + x3*(y1-y2)) / 2|.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N^3) — because we are generating all possible triangles and calculating their areas
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — because we are only using a constant amount of space to store the maximum area and the current area
